description: Figure 16 presents a cross-sectional SEM micrograph (a) and a corresponding manganese X-ray elemental map (b) of the oxide scale formed on the quaternary alloy Fe-15Mn-5Al-5Cr after isothermal oxidation at 800°C for 24 hr in pure oxygen at 200 torr. Image (a) reveals an extremely convoluted and detached oxide scale with uneven thickness, characteristic of breakaway oxidation behavior. The substrate surface became totally ferritic and manganese-depleted during oxidation. Image (b) demonstrates pronounced manganese enrichment in the thicker sections of the convoluted scale. Caption and citation text report aluminum enrichment at the scale base with iron and chromium concentrations qualitatively similar to the alloy matrix. These micrographs support the conclusion that austenite formation in duplex alloys promotes breakdown of preexisting protective alumina scales and growth of bulky manganese-rich oxides, resulting in convoluted scale morphologies and high oxidation weight gains (2.663 mg cm⁻² in 24 hr), despite chromium additions that typically reduce the aluminum threshold required for protective scale formation.
